ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Payment Of Surcharge
Off Calendar Sep-05-2017 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #6, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Aug-14-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Franchesca Callejo for and with May Wycoff (appeared by telephone).
Avid Alavi for Scott Hodges (Conservator for Michael Wall/Son of Francis).

Ms. Callejo stated that she does not know if she can add much to what has been filed, that they had to amend the accounting to go through date of death, that she looked at the objections, that she was told by her Client that they received individual receipts for the expenses, and that she received receipts for the 5th Account, but not for the sixth account. Ms. Callejo also stated that she believes the expenses are consistent, that she defers to the Court on the attorney's fees, that she is happy to return funds she is holding, that the delay was due to obtaining post death bank statements, spoke of her Clients age and the amount of stress she was dealing with, spoke of the need for Francis to have one on one attendant care, Francis' need for assistance with feeding, stated that Ms. Wycoff did not make detailed notes, that Ms. Wycoff's average amount of time was about four hours a month, that it is a reasonable estimate of time that she spent and that she feels her Clien did the best she could. Ms. Alavi stated that any funds remaining in the Trust would be a benefit to Michael, that Michael's rent is $400.00 a month short which her Client is currently paying, presented oral to support her position with respect to reimbursement for the accountant fees, reimbursement of payments to Ms. Cabusas, etc. The Amended Sixth and Final Report and Account, etc. is taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #6, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Apr-11-2017 Continued To Jun-06-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Franchesca Callejo for May Wycoff (Trustee).
Avid Alavi for Scott Hodges (Conservator of Michael Wall).

Ms. Callejo stated that they obtained statements from the Accountant that prepared the prior accounting, that they obtained bank records, that the documents were sent to the Accountant who will be working on it and that the Accountant would like three weeks. Ms. Callejo also stated that they will revise the accounting to go to date of death and then a final accounting post death through February 24th. The accounting petition is continued to June 6, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The amended petition is to be noticed for June 6, 2017.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #6, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Mar-14-2017 Continued To Apr-11-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Franchesca Callejo, for the Trustee, May Wycoff; Avid Alavi, for Scott Hodges, Trustee for the beneficiary, Michael Way.

Ms. Callejo reports on the status of the trust and accounting, noting that the trustee is 92 years old and that her 94 year old husband, who has assisted in the maintenance and administration of the trust these many years, is in poor health and possibly in extremis. She further notes that under the circumstances, the current accounting should probably be supplemented with a final accounting and the trust terminated. It is Ms. Callejo's understanding that there are only a few matters outstanding with the accounting but because of the age and circumstances of the Trustee, a continuance of some length would be required.

Ms. Alavi addresses the concerns of the trustee for the beneficiary/Conservatee Michael Way, noting that there are a number of outstanding accounting concerns and that the beneficiary Michael Way has been differently placed due to the deficits of the trust in question. She reports that the new placement seems to be working well. She also agrees that the current accounting should be supplemented for a final accounting.

After consideration, the Court continues the matter to April 11, 2017 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204 for further status although the Court strongly encourages Ms. Callejo to file a final accounting and a Petition to Terminate the Trust by that date.

Judge: Hon. Peter J. Busch; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Bailiff: Deputy Jaime; Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #5, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jan-19-2016 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #5, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Dec-08-2015 Continued To Jan-19-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Franchesca Callejo for petitioner May Wycoff; Lawrence Siracusa for Scott Hodges.

Ms. Callejo ststed that she plans to file an amendment to the petition by the end of next week, with the petitioner abandoning the 1% fee request and submitting time sheets and records for reimbursement in accordance with the terms of the trust agreement. The Court stated his concerns regarding the accounting as to third party charges. He also wanted to know plans for alternative placement in view of the fact that the money in the trust is running out. Mr. Siracusa stated that his client believes the trust has been mismanaged the trust, and that Mrs. Wall was not properly placed in an assisted living facility with 24-hour care. He believes that she should be in a skilled nursing care facility, which would be at a reduced cost from her current placement and would preserve limited trust funds for future care and for the residuary beneficiary of the trust, her son Francis Wall. Mr. Siracusa opined that it was improper for the trustee to spend down the limited trust funds without taking into account the future needs of the residuary trust beneficiary. The Court encouraged counsel to work together and try to reach a resolution of the dispute. The matter was continued to January 19, 2016 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not reported)

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (1926 34th Avenue, San Francisco, Ca 94121)
Off Calendar Oct-02-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Genevieve Callejo for May Waycoff (Trustee). David Chan (Agent for Ms. Yee) also present.

The Court stated that notice was sent in care of the Agent and notice was not given to Ms. Yee (Purchaser). Ms. Yee was not present. Mr. Chan represented to the Court that Ms. Yee gave him authority to be present. The sale was confirmed at $620,000.00 subject to the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition for Confirmation of Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). An additional bond in the amount of $648,000.00 is required.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR The Issue Of Sale Of The Real Property
Off Calendar Apr-11-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Franchesca Callejo for May Wycoff. Avid Alavi for Scott Hodges (Conservator). Michael Wall (son on Francis Wall)

Ms. Callejo reported that Ms. Wall is being moved to a new facility (Marymount) on April 20,2013 with a savings of a little less than $4,500.00 a month, stated that she was not sure as to why the care givers were willing to work for lower wages, and that her client made the changes to try to preserve the assets. She also reported that the cash on hand as of March was $167,000.00, that there estimated taxes, house insurance, estimated that there will be about $135,000.00 at the end of this month and that she expects that the house should be listed for sale as of June 1, 2013. Ms. Alavi agrees that it is appropriate to list the property by June 1, 2013, stated that it is her understanding that Ms. Wall is being moved to another assisted living facility rather than a skilled nursing facility and perhaps that is why two care givers are still needed. The Court granted authority to sell the real property (subject to review of the order). The order is to reflect that the listing is to commence on June 1, 2013.

Judge: Lillian K. Sing. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Nancy Bautista. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #4, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Feb-19-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Avid Alavi for and with Scott Hodges (Conservator for Michael Wall-Son of Conservatee). Franchesca Callejo for May Wycoff (Trustee/Petitioner).

The Parties spoke of the high costs of care for the Conservatee at the assisted living facility, the Conservatee's and Michael's medical condition, and also presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to sale of the property, the Conservatee being moved to a Skilled Nursing Facility and Michael's future living arrangement. The Court stated that it was not ready to make a decision today and that it needed more information. The Court asked Counsel to address the following: 1) Is a Case Manager still necessary? 2) Are two caregivers still required? Can that be reduced? What services are being provided at the assisted living facility? 3) Mr. Hodges is to look into what other living options are available to Michael. If the house is sold, where will Michael go? Ms. Callejo stated that she will have the Case Manager prepare a report. Reports regarding the above are due by April 3, 2013. The Court approved the accounting petition (subject to review of the revised order). The issue of sale of the real property was continued to April 10, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Lillian K. Sing. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.
